subject: Sociology
title: Marxist Theory of Education
description: Learn Marxist Theory of Education in one minute.

hook.text: Schools claim to give everyone an equal chance. Marxists say they're designed to do the opposite.

buildup.text: Bowles and Gintis argued schools mirror the capitalist workplace: hierarchy, obedience, and fragmented tasks.

discovery.text: Their correspondence principle says school structure corresponds to work structure, training compliant laborers.

twist.text: Working-class schools emphasize rule-following. Elite schools emphasize creativity and leadership — different futures.

climax.text: For Marxists, education doesn't overcome class — it reproduces it while claiming to offer equal opportunity.

punchline.text: Schools sort children by class origins.
